steps.title: Sample the scatterplot into a Desmos table
steps.content: This is a regression question, so the strongest shortcut is to approximate several plotted points and fit the model. From the scatterplot, enter about ten representative points into Desmos.

steps.title: Run a quadratic regression
steps.content: Type $y_1\sim ax_1^2+bx_1+c$. The regression gives a negative $a$, a positive $b$, and a positive $c$ close to $745.73$. That points to the model with a negative $x^2$ coefficient and positive constant.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choices A and C open upward because their $x^2$ coefficient is positive. Choice B opens downward but has a negative constant, putting the $y$-intercept far below the data. Choice D has the correct downward shape and positive intercept, so choice D is correct.
